rulings.Case Title: CORNELIUS MURPHY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 7, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendants Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc. and Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc.¿s motion to stay is granted with the following conditions [note the language edited by the court]: (1) The matter be stayed pending the ruling of the October 9, 2012 date set for hearing of defendants¿ motion to disqualify plaintiffs¿ counsel per defendants¿ request, and without prejudice to plaintiffs¿ right to oppose any further request for stay; (2) That plaintiffs¿ non-opposition to the requested stay not be deemed in any fashion an admission of the truth as to any purported fact or a concession of the correctness of any legal argument set forth in defendants¿ moving papers in connection to this motion; and (3) That plaintiffs¿ non-opposition to the requested stay shall not be deemed to concede that defendants¿ underlying motion to disqualify has any merit. The court will sign the proposed order already submitted by plaintiffs¿ counsel, with the court¿s edits, at the hearing, which will be at 11:15am at trial call, not at 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND BARRAGAN VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 7, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Golden Gate Drywall, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to negate an essential element of plaintiff¿s claims. Even if admissible, Mr. Madeiros¿ declaration, when read in conjunction with plaintiff¿s deposition testimony, merely creates a triable issue whether plaintiff worked at a site at which defendant also worked. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE PATTERSON et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 7, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant BW/IP, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Patterson was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar because defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs submitted factually devoid substantive responses, as opposed to only objections, to contention discovery designed to elicit all of plaintiffs¿ evidence in support of their claims against defendant. Defendant failed to negate an essential element of plaintiffs¿ claims. Plaintiffs¿ objections to Mr. Costanzo¿s declaration are sustained. Even if admissible, Mr. Costanzo¿s declaration does not negate an essential element of plaintiffs¿ claims. The court only considered this as a motion for summary judgment. Although defendant¿s caption titles it a motion for summary judgment or in the alternative motion for summary adjudication, defendant¿s notice and separate statement failed to state the specific causes of action or issues sought to be adjudicated in violation of CRC 3.1350(b), (d). Even if the court considers defendant¿s separate statement in support of its motion for summary judgment to apply to issues sought to be summarily adjudicated, defendant¿s separate statement did not make an issue of false representation, intentional tort, loss of consortium or punitive damages. Plaintiffs¿ request for sanctions is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
